controls.title: Account Management
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures accounts are approved, provisioned, changed, and removed through controlled lifecycle workflows. The platform supports that objective through invitation and policy controls, SCIM-backed provisioning paths, member-state lifecycle handling, and auditable membership events in the hosted service.
controls.controlStatement: Define and document the types of accounts allowed and specifically prohibited for use within the system; Assign account managers; Require {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.01 }} for group and role membership; Specify: Authorized users of the system; Group and role membership; and Access authorizations (i.e., privileges) and {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.02 }} for each account; Require approvals by {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.03 }} for requests to create accounts; Create, enable, modify, disable, and remove accounts in accordance with {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.04 }}; Monitor the use of accounts; Notify account managers and {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.05 }} within: {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.06 }} when accounts are no longer required; {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.07 }} when users are terminated or transferred; and {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.08 }} when system usage or need-to-know changes for an individual; Authorize access to the system based on: A valid access authorization; Intended system usage; and {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.09 }}; Review accounts for compliance with account management requirements {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.10 }}; Establish and implement a process for changing shared or group account authenticators (if deployed) when individuals are removed from the group; and Align account management processes with personnel termination and transfer processes.

platformChecklistItems.label: Account approval and role assignment controls exist
platformChecklistItems.description: Account creation and privileged role assignment should flow through authorized invitation, provisioning, or administrative paths.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Invitation, provisioning, and role-assignment workflow review

seed.notes: Invitation policies, admin-managed member changes, and SCIM lifecycle paths provide controlled account creation and role assignment paths.

evidence.title: Organization invitation and policy controls
evidence.description: convex/organizationManagement.ts enforces invitePolicy, verifiedDomainsOnly, and memberCap controls before new organization access is granted.

evidence.title: Controlled member role updates
evidence.description: convex/organizationManagement.ts resolves organization access context before member role changes and provisioning actions proceed.

platformChecklistItems.label: Account lifecycle controls are implemented
platformChecklistItems.description: Invitations, provisioning, suspension, deactivation, and reactivation workflows must support controlled onboarding and offboarding.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Organization management and provisioning workflow review

seed.notes: Organization policies constrain invitations and seat growth, while SCIM and member-state workflows support lifecycle changes.

evidence.title: Organization access policy controls
evidence.description: convex/organizationManagement.ts enforces invitePolicy, verifiedDomainsOnly, and memberCap policy checks; src/features/organizations/components/OrganizationPoliciesCard.tsx exposes the management surface.

evidence.title: SCIM provisioning workflow
evidence.description: src/features/organizations/components/OrganizationProvisioningManagement.tsx and src/features/organizations/server/organization-management.ts manage SCIM endpoint and bearer-token lifecycle for automated provisioning.

platformChecklistItems.label: Membership changes are auditable
platformChecklistItems.description: Administrative membership changes must emit reviewable audit records.

seed.notes: Membership lifecycle events are emitted as audit events and reviewable through organization audit history.

evidence.title: Organization membership audit events
evidence.description: src/lib/shared/auth-audit.ts defines member_added, member_removed, member_role_updated, member_suspended, member_deactivated, member_reactivated, and SCIM lifecycle event types.

evidence.title: Organization audit review surface
evidence.description: src/features/organizations/components/OrganizationAuditPage.tsx exposes membership-related audit history to organization owners, admins, and site admins.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for workforce onboarding, termination, role assignment approval, and periodic access review.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement policies and procedures to ensure that all members of its workforce have appropriate access to electronic protected health information, as provided under paragraph (a)(4) of this section, and to prevent those workforce members who do not have access under paragraph (a)(4) of this section from obtaining access to electronic protected health information.
HIPAA Standard: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to electronic protected health information that are consistent with the applicable requirements of subpart E of this part.

csf20.label: PR / PR.AA: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization
PR / PR.AA: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity. For those users whose access is administered by the entity, user system credentials are removed when user access is no longer authorized.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures users and processes can perform only the actions and data access they are authorized to use. The platform addresses that objective primarily through server-side authorization checks, organization-scoped permission decisions, and protected route guards that prevent accidental exposure in the user interface.
controls.controlStatement: Access Enforcement is tracked as an active control in the platform register.

platformChecklistItems.label: Protected routes are guarded
platformChecklistItems.description: Protected pages must require authorized access before rendering sensitive content.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Route guard review and route-level test coverage

seed.notes: Protected routes are guarded through shared auth and admin guard utilities.

evidence.title: Shared route guards
evidence.description: src/features/auth/server/route-guards.ts and auth-guards.ts provide requireAuth and requireAdmin guard paths used across protected flows.

platformChecklistItems.label: Server authorization checks enforce access
platformChecklistItems.description: Server-side actions and data access must enforce role-appropriate authorization.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Server function inspection and automated tests

seed.notes: Server-side access checks rely on shared auth guards, site-admin wrappers, and organization permission decisions before sensitive actions proceed.

evidence.title: Server auth guard enforcement
evidence.description: src/features/auth/server/auth-guards.ts implements requireAuth and requireAdmin for protected server operations.

evidence.title: Authorization builders and access decisions
evidence.description: convex/auth/authorized.ts and convex/auth/access.ts provide protected builders and role-aware access decisions for sensitive server paths.

platformChecklistItems.label: Organization-scoped permissions restrict actions
platformChecklistItems.description: Organization membership, role, and policy context must constrain who can manage organization resources.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Organization access context and permission helper review

seed.notes: Organization access is evaluated against viewer role, membership state, and policy gates before privileged actions are allowed.

evidence.title: Organization permission helpers
evidence.description: src/features/organizations/lib/organization-permissions.ts defines organization capability rules used across directory and policy workflows.

evidence.title: Organization access enforcement
evidence.description: convex/organizationManagement.ts resolves organization access context and rejects unauthorized member, policy, and provisioning mutations.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for defining roles, approving privileged access, and maintaining least-privilege assignments within the service.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to electronic protected health information that are consistent with the applicable requirements of subpart E of this part.

csf20.label: PR / PR.AA: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties, to meet the entity’s objectives.

controls.title: Least Privilege
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures privileged access is limited to the minimum set of roles, actions, and conditions necessary for administrative or organization-scoped duties. The platform supports that objective through explicit role separation, organization-scoped permission checks, and stronger assurance requirements for elevated actions.
controls.controlStatement: Least Privilege is tracked as an active control in the platform register.

platformChecklistItems.label: Privileged roles are explicitly defined and separated by responsibility
platformChecklistItems.description: Role definitions should distinguish site-admin, organization-owner, organization-admin, and member capabilities.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Role model and authorization helper review

seed.notes: Privileged access is separated across site-admin, owner, admin, and member roles with explicit capability boundaries.

evidence.title: Organization role and capability model
evidence.description: src/features/organizations/lib/organization-permissions.ts defines site-admin, owner, admin, and member access boundaries.

evidence.title: Authorized Convex builders
evidence.description: convex/auth/authorized.ts separates organization-scoped and site-admin query, mutation, and action wrappers.

platformChecklistItems.label: Admin and organization-management actions enforce least-privilege authorization
platformChecklistItems.description: Administrative and organization-management flows must enforce the narrowest allowed action set for the acting role.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Authorization path review and permission decision inspection

seed.notes: Server-side guards, authorization builders, and organization permission decisions enforce role-appropriate access before privileged actions proceed.

evidence.title: Privileged server access guards
evidence.description: src/features/auth/server/auth-guards.ts requires authenticated access and site-admin checks before privileged server flows.

evidence.title: Organization permission decision logic
evidence.description: convex/auth/access.ts evaluates membership state, viewer role, enterprise constraints, and permission-specific access before allowing organization actions.

platformChecklistItems.label: High-risk actions require additional protections such as MFA or recent step-up
platformChecklistItems.description: High-risk operations should require stronger assurance such as MFA enrollment or recent step-up verification.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Auth policy and fresh-session protection review

seed.notes: Site-admin access requires MFA or passkeys, and sensitive actions can require recent step-up verification.

evidence.title: MFA enforcement for site-admin access
evidence.description: convex/auth/access.ts and src/features/auth/server/auth-guards.ts reject site-admin access when MFA or passkey requirements are not satisfied.

evidence.title: Recent step-up protections
evidence.description: src/lib/shared/security-baseline.ts requires step-up for audit exports and src/features/auth/server/auth-guards.ts redirects users through the fresh-session flow when needed.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for granting roles appropriately, reviewing privileged assignments, and limiting who receives elevated access within their tenant.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to electronic protected health information that are consistent with the applicable requirements of subpart E of this part.

csf20.label: PR / PR.AA: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties, to meet the entity’s objectives.

controls.title: Audit Record Generation
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures the platform generates audit records for defined security-relevant events across application and backend workflows. The platform supports that objective through a canonical event inventory and event emission across authentication, administrative, evidence, and file-handling paths.
controls.controlStatement: Provide audit record generation capability for the event types the system is capable of auditing as defined in [AU-2a](#au-2_smt.a) on {{ insert: param, au-12_odp.01 }}; Allow {{ insert: param, au-12_odp.02 }} to select the event types that are to be logged by specific components of the system; and Generate audit records for the event types defined in [AU-2c](#au-2_smt.c) that include the audit record content defined in [AU-3](#au-3).

platformChecklistItems.label: Security-relevant events are explicitly defined in the audit event inventory
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should maintain an explicit inventory of audit-relevant event types.

seed.notes: A shared event inventory defines the security-relevant audit events used throughout the app.

evidence.title: Canonical auth and security event inventory
evidence.description: src/lib/shared/auth-audit.ts enumerates authentication, organization, evidence, attachment, vendor, and step-up audit event types.

platformChecklistItems.label: Evidence, file-handling, vendor, and related security workflows emit audit records
platformChecklistItems.description: Core security-sensitive workflows should emit audit records during normal and failed operations.

seed.notes: Attachment inspection, evidence handling, and vendor access paths emit audit records for review.

evidence.title: Attachment inspection audit events
evidence.description: convex/agentChatActions.ts emits chat_attachment_scan_passed, chat_attachment_scan_failed, and chat_attachment_quarantined events.

evidence.title: Evidence and vendor audit events
evidence.description: convex/security.ts emits evidence_report_generated, evidence_report_exported, and evidence_report_reviewed while convex/agentChatActions.ts emits outbound vendor access events.

platformChecklistItems.label: Audit generation is exercised through the application and Convex server paths
platformChecklistItems.description: Audit generation should be available from client-triggered workflows and backend Convex operations.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Client and backend audit path review

seed.notes: Both client-driven flows and backend actions route events through shared audit insertion paths.

evidence.title: Client audit event action
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts provides recordClientAuditEvent for authenticated client-triggered audit emission.

evidence.title: Backend audit insertion
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts exposes insertAuditLog and multiple Convex actions and mutations call it during protected workflows.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for determining which exported audit records they retain externally and how those records are integrated into their broader oversight processes.

nist80066.label: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.
HIPAA Standard: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

csf20.label: PR / PR.PS: Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring
DE / DE.CM: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
DE / DE.CM: Personnel activity and technology usage are monitored to find potentially adverse events

soc2.label: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.

controls.title: Event Logging
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures security-relevant events are identified and recorded with enough context to support oversight. The platform addresses that objective by capturing audit events for authentication, administrative, organization, evidence, and file-handling activity and by exposing those records to authorized reviewers.
controls.controlStatement: Identify the types of events that the system is capable of logging in support of the audit function: {{ insert: param, au-02_odp.01 }}; Coordinate the event logging function with other organizational entities requiring audit-related information to guide and inform the selection criteria for events to be logged; Specify the following event types for logging within the system: {{ insert: param, au-2_prm_2 }}; Provide a rationale for why the event types selected for logging are deemed to be adequate to support after-the-fact investigations of incidents; and Review and update the event types selected for logging {{ insert: param, au-02_odp.04 }}.

platformChecklistItems.label: Security-relevant events are captured
platformChecklistItems.description: Authentication, administrative, and security-significant events must be recorded.

seed.notes: Audit event types cover authentication, organization, attachment, and evidence-report activity.

evidence.title: Audit event inventory
evidence.description: src/lib/shared/auth-audit.ts defines a broad set of security-relevant audit event types.

platformChecklistItems.label: Authorized reviewers can inspect audit records
platformChecklistItems.description: Authorized reviewers must be able to inspect captured audit activity through supported review surfaces.

seed.notes: The platform exposes audit history views to authorized reviewers in organization and security-admin surfaces.

evidence.title: Organization audit review UI
evidence.description: src/features/organizations/components/OrganizationAuditPage.tsx provides audit history review for authorized organization roles.

evidence.title: Security admin audit review surface
evidence.description: src/routes/app/admin/security.tsx exposes recent security signals and linked evidence for authorized administrative review.

platformChecklistItems.label: Audit records retain actor and event context
platformChecklistItems.description: Recorded audit entries should retain event, actor, and related resource context needed for review.

seed.notes: Audit records carry event metadata and resource context that reviewers can inspect later.

evidence.title: Structured audit record insertion
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts records eventType, actor, resource metadata, and serialized event metadata when audit entries are inserted.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for reviewing exported audit records and aligning retention or downstream log handling with their own policies when those records leave the platform.

nist80066.label: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.
HIPAA Standard: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

csf20.label: PR / PR.PS: Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring

soc2.label: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.

controls.title: Configuration Settings
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures regulated security settings are defined centrally and enforced consistently across the hosted service. The platform supports that objective through centrally defined regulated defaults, fail-closed auth configuration validation, and explicit secure session settings in the auth runtime.
controls.controlStatement: Establish and document configuration settings for components employed within the system that reflect the most restrictive mode consistent with operational requirements using {{ insert: param, cm-06_odp.01 }}; Implement the configuration settings; Identify, document, and approve any deviations from established configuration settings for {{ insert: param, cm-06_odp.02 }} based on {{ insert: param, cm-06_odp.03 }} ; and Monitor and control changes to the configuration settings in accordance with organizational policies and procedures.

platformChecklistItems.label: Regulated security defaults are defined centrally and enforced consistently
platformChecklistItems.description: Regulated baseline defaults should be defined centrally and applied consistently to tenant-facing policy settings.

seed.notes: The regulated baseline centralizes retention, MFA, verification, and step-up defaults for protected service behavior.

evidence.title: Central regulated baseline defaults
evidence.description: src/lib/shared/security-baseline.ts defines regulated retention defaults, always-on baseline requirements, and organization policy defaults.

platformChecklistItems.label: Authentication and session settings fail closed when insecure configuration is supplied
platformChecklistItems.description: Auth and session configuration should reject insecure or malformed settings instead of silently degrading security.

seed.notes: The auth runtime rejects malformed origins and enforces secure session configuration for regulated deployments.

evidence.title: Fail-closed Better Auth env validation
evidence.description: src/lib/server/env.server.ts rejects invalid BETTER_AUTH_URL, preview host, and trusted-origin configuration at startup.

evidence.title: Explicit secure session settings
evidence.description: convex/betterAuth/sharedOptions.ts sets session expiry, refresh, freshness, database-backed sessions, and disables cookie cache for security-sensitive revocation behavior.

platformChecklistItems.label: Security-sensitive runtime configuration is validated before the app starts
platformChecklistItems.description: Security-sensitive runtime settings should be validated during startup so unsafe configuration is rejected early.

seed.notes: Security-sensitive runtime configuration is validated for auth origins and vendor configuration before protected paths proceed.

evidence.title: Auth and site URL validation
evidence.description: src/lib/server/env.server.ts validates security-sensitive auth and site URL inputs as canonical origins before use.

evidence.title: Vendor runtime validation
evidence.description: src/lib/server/openrouter.ts validates OpenRouter privacy mode and API key presence before outbound model use.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for safe configuration of any customer-controlled integrations, identity providers, or downstream policies they connect to the platform.

nist80066.label: Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level to comply with §164.306(a)
HIPAA Standard: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed.

csf20.label: PR / PR.PS: Configuration management practices are established and applied

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures users are uniquely identified and authenticated before accessing protected service functionality. The platform supports that objective through authenticated access flows, verified-email enforcement, MFA or passkey enforcement for regulated access, and fresh-session step-up for sensitive operations.
controls.controlStatement: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users) is tracked as an active control in the platform register.

platformChecklistItems.label: Authenticated access is required
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform must require authenticated access for protected user areas.

seed.notes: Protected application use depends on authenticated sessions and server-side identity checks before access is granted.

evidence.title: Authenticated access guardrails
evidence.description: src/features/auth/server/auth-guards.ts and route-guards.ts gate protected access while convex/auth/access.ts resolves authenticated user context for server-side authorization.

evidence.title: Auth runtime session handling
evidence.description: convex/betterAuth/sharedOptions.ts configures the Better Auth server plugin set and session handling used for authenticated access.

platformChecklistItems.label: Verified email checks are enforced
platformChecklistItems.description: Production authentication flows must enforce verified-email expectations where required.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Policy inspection and auth configuration review

seed.notes: Verified email is enforced before protected access and is part of the regulated baseline.

evidence.title: Verified email enforcement
evidence.description: src/features/auth/server/auth-guards.ts redirects unverified accounts away from protected access and src/lib/shared/security-baseline.ts requires verified email by default.

platformChecklistItems.label: MFA or passkeys are enforced for regulated access
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform must enforce stronger authenticators for privileged or regulated organization access paths.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Authentication policy and step-up workflow review

seed.notes: The regulated baseline requires MFA or passkeys, and privileged access paths enforce that requirement.

evidence.title: Regulated MFA baseline
evidence.description: src/lib/shared/security-baseline.ts requires MFA or passkeys for regulated organizations and src/features/organizations/components/OrganizationPoliciesCard.tsx presents the always-enforced posture.

evidence.title: MFA and fresh-session enforcement
evidence.description: src/features/auth/server/auth-guards.ts and convex/organizationManagement.ts require MFA/passkey presence or a fresh session before privileged access and regulated join flows.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for identity proofing, MFA policy decisions, user enrollment expectations, and account lifecycle governance.

nist80066.label: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.
HIPAA Standard: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed.

csf20.label: PR / PR.AA: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization
PR / PR.AA: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

controls.title: Protection of Information at Rest
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures information stored within the service boundary is protected against unauthorized access or alteration at rest. The platform addresses that objective through managed encrypted storage, blocked public access, and controlled file access for data managed within the service boundary.
controls.controlStatement: Protection of Information at Rest is tracked as an active control in the platform register.

platformChecklistItems.label: Hosted storage protections are configured
platformChecklistItems.description: Data at rest protections must be configured for hosted storage within the service boundary.

seed.notes: Managed storage configuration enforces blocked public access, server-side encryption, and versioning for protected data at rest.

evidence.title: Encrypted S3 storage configuration
evidence.description: infra/aws-cdk/lib/malware-scan-stack.cts provisions an S3 bucket with BLOCK_ALL public access, S3-managed encryption, enforceSSL, object ownership enforcement, and versioning.

platformChecklistItems.label: Access to stored protected files is controlled
platformChecklistItems.description: Access to stored protected files should rely on controlled serve paths rather than open object access.

seed.notes: Protected files are served through controlled signed paths instead of open storage object access.

evidence.title: Controlled protected file access
evidence.description: convex/storagePlatform.ts and convex/fileServing.ts route protected file access through signed serve paths instead of direct open object reads.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for governing exported data, retention obligations they impose on platform use, and any external storage or integrations they control.

nist80066.label: Consider whether the addressable implementation specifications of this standard are reasonable and appropriate:

csf20.label: PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

controls.title: Boundary Protection
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures protected functions, files, and tenant-scoped resources are only accessible through approved boundary paths. The platform supports that objective through trusted-origin validation, signed file-serving paths, scoped storage access checks, and organization-boundary authorization logic.
controls.controlStatement: Monitor and control communications at the external managed interfaces to the system and at key internal managed interfaces within the system; Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components that are {{ insert: param, sc-07_odp }} separated from internal organizational networks; and Connect to external networks or systems only through managed interfaces consisting of boundary protection devices arranged in accordance with an organizational security and privacy architecture.

platformChecklistItems.label: Trusted-origin and boundary checks restrict inbound access to approved origins
platformChecklistItems.description: Authentication and other boundary-sensitive request paths should reject unapproved origins and malformed boundary configuration.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Origin validation and auth middleware review

seed.notes: The auth layer fails closed on invalid origin configuration and rejects requests from untrusted origins before credential handling.

evidence.title: Trusted-origin validation helpers
evidence.description: src/lib/server/env.server.ts validates BETTER_AUTH_URL, preview hosts, and trusted origins as canonical approved origins.

evidence.title: Auth middleware origin rejection
evidence.description: convex/betterAuth/sharedOptions.ts rejects authentication requests from untrusted origins before processing credentials.

platformChecklistItems.label: Signed file-serving and scoped access paths protect boundary crossings
platformChecklistItems.description: Boundary-crossing file access should rely on signatures and scoped authorization rather than open object access.

seed.notes: File serving requires scoped read authorization and signed serve URLs, and quarantined files are blocked.

evidence.title: Signed file-serving implementation
evidence.description: convex/fileServing.ts signs serve URLs with HMAC and blocks quarantined or infected files before redirecting to storage.

evidence.title: Scoped storage read authorization
evidence.description: convex/auth/access.ts resolves storage read access and logs authorization denial before rejecting unauthorized reads.

platformChecklistItems.label: Tenant and admin boundaries prevent unauthorized cross-scope access
platformChecklistItems.description: Tenant-scoped and admin-scoped operations should reject unauthorized cross-scope access and route privileged operations through the correct boundary.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Tenant authorization and SCIM path review

seed.notes: Organization membership, viewer role, and admin wrappers constrain cross-scope access, including SCIM management boundaries.

evidence.title: Tenant and viewer-role permission decisions
evidence.description: convex/auth/access.ts resolves organization permission decisions using membership, viewer role, and enterprise access requirements.

evidence.title: Org-scoped SCIM boundary controls
evidence.description: convex/betterAuth/sharedOptions.ts blocks direct Better Auth SCIM deletion and routes SCIM management through org-scoped access checks.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for boundary controls in their own networks, browsers, devices, and downstream integrations that connect to the hosted service.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to electronic protected health information that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.

csf20.label: PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected
PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected
PR / PR.IR: Networks and environments are protected from unauthorized logical access and usage

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.

The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal to meet the entity’s objectives.

controls.title: Malicious Code Protection
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures files entering protected workflows are inspected for unsafe characteristics and contained when suspicious or infected conditions are detected. The platform supports that objective through built-in file inspection, quarantine and rejection paths, malware-finding ingestion, and downstream containment actions on affected files.
controls.controlStatement: Implement {{ insert: param, si-03_odp.01 }} malicious code protection mechanisms at system entry and exit points to detect and eradicate malicious code; Automatically update malicious code protection mechanisms as new releases are available in accordance with organizational configuration management policy and procedures; Configure malicious code protection mechanisms to: Perform periodic scans of the system {{ insert: param, si-03_odp.02 }} and real-time scans of files from external sources at {{ insert: param, si-03_odp.03 }} as the files are downloaded, opened, or executed in accordance with organizational policy; and {{ insert: param, si-03_odp.04 }} ; and send alert to {{ insert: param, si-03_odp.06 }} in response to malicious code detection; and Address the receipt of false positives during malicious code detection and eradication and the resulting potential impact on the availability of the system.

platformChecklistItems.label: Uploaded files are inspected before being accepted into protected workflows
platformChecklistItems.description: Protected document and attachment workflows should inspect file content characteristics before acceptance.

seed.notes: Uploaded files are inspected for type, size, and signature mismatches before protected workflows accept them.

evidence.title: Built-in file inspection pipeline
evidence.description: src/lib/server/file-inspection.server.ts validates file kind, size limits, and signature matches before returning accepted status.

evidence.title: Attachment scan event recording
evidence.description: convex/agentChatActions.ts records document scan events for inspected attachments before downstream chat workflows continue.

platformChecklistItems.label: Suspicious or infected files are quarantined or rejected automatically
platformChecklistItems.description: Unsafe files should be quarantined or rejected automatically when inspection or malware findings indicate risk.

seed.notes: Signature mismatches are quarantined, unsupported files are rejected, and malware findings trigger quarantine.

evidence.title: Quarantine and rejection decisions
evidence.description: src/lib/server/file-inspection.server.ts returns quarantined or rejected results for unsafe file conditions.

evidence.title: Attachment quarantine mutation flow
evidence.description: convex/agentChatActions.ts marks affected attachments quarantined or rejected and records the reason for later review.

platformChecklistItems.label: Malware findings are recorded and can trigger downstream containment actions
platformChecklistItems.description: Malware findings should be recorded with enough context to drive downstream containment and review.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Malware finding ingestion and storage review

seed.notes: GuardDuty malware findings are verified, recorded, and used to mark files infected and quarantine them.

evidence.title: Signed GuardDuty webhook verification
evidence.description: convex/storageWebhook.ts verifies webhook signatures and timestamps before applying GuardDuty malware findings.

evidence.title: Malware finding persistence
evidence.description: convex/schema.ts stores malware status, finding IDs, and quarantine timestamps on storageLifecycle records used for containment and review.

evidence.title: Malware scanning infrastructure
evidence.description: infra/aws-cdk/lib/malware-scan-stack.cts provisions the GuardDuty malware protection plan, result forwarding Lambda, and protected S3 bucket.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for broader endpoint and email malware defenses outside the hosted file-ingest paths provided by the platform.

nist80066.label: Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI held by the covered entity or business associate.
Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level to comply with §164.306(a)

soc2.label: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software to meet the entity’s objectives.

controls.title: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures important service data and content flows retain integrity signals that help detect mismatches, tampering, or unsafe alteration. The platform supports that objective through file signature validation, hash-linked audit records, and integrity-linked evidence and signed file-serving flows.
controls.controlStatement: Employ integrity verification tools to detect unauthorized changes to the following software, firmware, and information: {{ insert: param, si-7_prm_1 }} ; and Take the following actions when unauthorized changes to the software, firmware, and information are detected: {{ insert: param, si-7_prm_2 }}.

platformChecklistItems.label: File type and signature validation protect against content integrity mismatches
platformChecklistItems.description: Protected file workflows should validate content signatures against declared type or extension expectations.

seed.notes: File inspection validates content signatures and quarantines mismatches before protected processing continues.

evidence.title: Signature and kind validation
evidence.description: src/lib/server/file-inspection.server.ts resolves known file kinds and checks signatures before accepting files.

platformChecklistItems.label: Audit data integrity is verified through chained hashes or equivalent controls
platformChecklistItems.description: Audit records should carry linked integrity values and be verifiable after creation.

seed.notes: Audit events are linked through chained hashes and regularly verifiable through a dedicated integrity check path.

evidence.title: Audit hash chain
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts computes eventHash values using previousEventHash so audit records form a verifiable chain.

evidence.title: Audit chain verification action
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts provides verifyAuditIntegrityInternal to recompute hashes and detect integrity failures.

platformChecklistItems.label: Integrity-protecting mechanisms exist for exported evidence and signed file access flows
platformChecklistItems.description: Evidence exports and signed file access paths should include integrity-preserving metadata or signatures that can be validated later.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Evidence export and signed file access review

seed.notes: Evidence exports preserve content and export hashes, and signed file-serving paths protect storage access flows.

evidence.title: Evidence export integrity metadata
evidence.description: convex/security.ts computes contentHash and exportHash values and stores exportIntegritySummary for evidence report exports.

evidence.title: Signed file serve URLs
evidence.description: convex/fileServing.ts generates HMAC-backed signed serve URLs for protected file access.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for validating the integrity of any exported material after it leaves the service and for protecting customer-managed downstream storage or transmission paths.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

csf20.label: PR / PR.PS: Software is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk

soc2.label: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software to meet the entity’s objectives.

controls.title: Protection of Audit Information
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures audit information is protected from unauthorized modification, monitored for tampering, and preserved with integrity metadata when shared or exported. The platform supports those objectives through access-controlled audit views, hash-linked audit records, and integrity-linked evidence report exports, while immutable retention controls beyond the application layer are not yet fully evidenced here.
controls.controlStatement: Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion; and Alert {{ insert: param, au-09_odp }} upon detection of unauthorized access, modification, or deletion of audit information.

platformChecklistItems.label: Audit records are protected against unauthorized modification or deletion
platformChecklistItems.description: Access to audit records should be restricted and managed to reduce unauthorized modification or deletion risk.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Audit access path and storage protection review

seed.notes: Audit access is constrained to privileged or self-scoped queries, but immutable storage or deletion-prevention evidence beyond app-layer controls is not attached yet.

evidence.title: Restricted audit query access
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts limits audit-log queries so non-admin users can only read their own records.

evidence.title: Privileged audit export path
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts restricts security audit export to verified site-admin users.

platformChecklistItems.label: Audit integrity checks detect broken hash chains or tampering conditions
platformChecklistItems.description: The audit subsystem should detect tampering conditions through integrity verification or equivalent controls.

seed.notes: Audit events are hash-linked and the platform can detect and record chain verification failures.

evidence.title: Hash-linked audit insertion
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts stores previousEventHash and eventHash when new audit records are inserted.

evidence.title: Audit integrity verification
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts recomputes the audit chain and emits audit_integrity_check_failed when hashes do not verify.

platformChecklistItems.label: Audit-supporting exports preserve integrity metadata and review state
platformChecklistItems.description: Exported audit-supporting evidence should retain integrity metadata and reviewer state that can be validated later.

seed.notes: Evidence reports retain content hashes, export hashes, integrity summaries, and review metadata for later verification.

evidence.title: Evidence report integrity fields
evidence.description: convex/schema.ts defines contentHash, exportHash, exportIntegritySummary, reviewStatus, reviewedAt, and reviewedByUserId on evidenceReports.

evidence.title: Integrity-linked export workflow
evidence.description: convex/security.ts generates export bundles with content and export hashes, and src/routes/app/admin/security.tsx surfaces those values to reviewers.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for protecting exported audit material after it leaves the service and for applying any downstream retention or immutability requirements they require.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

csf20.label: PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected

controls.title: System Backup
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures service data and required system information are backed up, protected, and recoverable after disruption or loss. For the hosted service, that means operator-run backup and restore capability for the production environment; this workspace currently evidences backup-verification recordkeeping, but operator backup configuration and restore-test evidence are not yet fully attached here.
controls.controlStatement: Conduct backups of user-level information contained in {{ insert: param, cp-09_odp.01 }} {{ insert: param, cp-09_odp.02 }}; Conduct backups of system-level information contained in the system {{ insert: param, cp-09_odp.03 }}; Conduct backups of system documentation, including security- and privacy-related documentation {{ insert: param, cp-09_odp.04 }} ; and Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of backup information.

platformChecklistItems.label: Hosted service backups are configured
platformChecklistItems.description: Provider-operated backups for production service data and essential system information should be defined and enabled.

seed.notes: The control register does not yet include provider backup configuration evidence for the hosted production environment.

platformChecklistItems.label: Backup verification records are maintained
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should retain verification outcomes or supporting records for completed backup checks and recovery validation.

seed.notes: The platform includes a dedicated backup verification record path, but this workspace does not yet contain retained hosted-environment verification entries.

evidence.title: Backup verification record path
evidence.description: convex/schema.ts defines backupVerificationReports and convex/security.ts exposes recordBackupVerification for storing backup and restore verification outcomes.

platformChecklistItems.label: Restore testing is performed and recorded
platformChecklistItems.description: Provider-operated restore tests and recovery validation results should be documented for the hosted service.

seed.notes: The platform has a restore-verification record shape and audit events, but hosted-environment restore drill results are not attached in this workspace yet.

evidence.title: Restore verification data model
evidence.description: convex/schema.ts stores restore_verification drill records and convex/security.ts records backup_restore_drill_completed and backup_restore_drill_failed audit events.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for determining whether the hosted service backup and recovery posture satisfies their retention, recovery-time, and business continuity requirements, and for protecting any data they export or replicate outside the service.

nist80066.label: Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of ePHI.
Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to restore any loss of data.

csf20.label: PR / PR.DS: Backups of data are created, protected, maintained, and tested
RC / RC.RP: The integrity of backups and other restoration assets is verified before using them for restoration

soc2.label: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data back-up processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its objectives.

controls.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
controls.implementationSummary: This control addresses vulnerability and security finding identification. The app ships automated inspection and malware-finding hooks for file-ingest surfaces, but hosted-service vulnerability scanning cadence, remediation tracking, and formal risk treatment still require operator processes outside this repo-backed workspace.
controls.controlStatement: Monitor and scan for vulnerabilities in the system and hosted applications {{ insert: param, ra-5_prm_1 }} and when new vulnerabilities potentially affecting the system are identified and reported; Employ vulnerability monitoring tools and techniques that facilitate interoperability among tools and automate parts of the vulnerability management process by using standards for: Enumerating platforms, software flaws, and improper configurations; Formatting checklists and test procedures; and Measuring vulnerability impact; Analyze vulnerability scan reports and results from vulnerability monitoring; Remediate legitimate vulnerabilities {{ insert: param, ra-05_odp.03 }} in accordance with an organizational assessment of risk; Share information obtained from the vulnerability monitoring process and control assessments with {{ insert: param, ra-05_odp.04 }} to help eliminate similar vulnerabilities in other systems; and Employ vulnerability monitoring tools that include the capability to readily update the vulnerabilities to be scanned.

platformChecklistItems.label: Automated security scanning hooks exist
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should implement automated inspection or malware-finding hooks for security-relevant ingest paths.

seed.notes: The platform has built-in file inspection and GuardDuty webhook integration for file-ingest surfaces, but that is not full hosted-service vulnerability-management evidence.

evidence.title: GuardDuty malware finding pipeline
evidence.description: infra/aws-cdk/lib/malware-scan-stack.cts provisions S3 malware scanning and convex/storageWebhook.ts verifies signed findings before quarantining affected files.

platformChecklistItems.label: Remediation and risk treatment are tracked
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform must retain remediation tracking or risk acceptance artifacts.

seed.notes: No remediation-tracking or risk-acceptance artifact is attached from the codebase.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for addressing vulnerabilities in their own endpoints, identity stores, integrations, and operational environments connected to the service.

nist80066.label: Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI held by the covered entity or business associate.
Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level to comply with §164.306(a)

csf20.label: ID / ID.RA: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded

controls.title: External System Services
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures externally provided services are governed by defined boundary expectations, permitted data classes, and auditable usage. The platform supports that objective through a vendor boundary registry, explicit allowed-data-class policies, approval gates, and audit events for vendor use or denial, while buyer-facing contractual and subprocessor program artifacts remain outside this repo-backed register.
controls.controlStatement: Require that providers of external system services comply with organizational security and privacy requirements and employ the following controls: {{ insert: param, sa-09_odp.01 }}; Define and document organizational oversight and user roles and responsibilities with regard to external system services; and Employ the following processes, methods, and techniques to monitor control compliance by external service providers on an ongoing basis: {{ insert: param, sa-09_odp.02 }}.

platformChecklistItems.label: External services and subprocessors are inventoried with approved data classes
platformChecklistItems.description: Approved external services should be recorded with the categories of data the service is permitted to handle, even if broader legal subprocessor records live elsewhere.

seed.notes: The repo models approved vendors, environments, and allowed data classes, but it is not yet a full buyer-facing subprocessor register with legal and assurance metadata.

evidence.title: Vendor boundary registry
evidence.description: src/lib/shared/vendor-boundary.ts inventories approved vendors, allowed data classes, allowed environments, and approval flags.

evidence.title: Runtime vendor posture snapshot
evidence.description: src/lib/server/vendor-boundary.server.ts exposes vendor approval status, allowed data classes, and environment posture for review surfaces.

platformChecklistItems.label: Outbound access to vendors is constrained by policy and environment
platformChecklistItems.description: Outbound service use should be constrained by allowed environments, approval gates, and permitted data classes.

seed.notes: Vendor egress is blocked when the vendor is not approved, the environment is not allowed, or the data classes exceed policy.

evidence.title: Vendor boundary enforcement helper
evidence.description: src/lib/server/vendor-boundary.server.ts rejects outbound access when vendor approval, environment, or data-class policy is not satisfied.

evidence.title: Outbound service call guardrails
evidence.description: src/lib/server/openrouter.ts and convex/emails.ts invoke assertVendorBoundary before outbound vendor access.

platformChecklistItems.label: Vendor usage is auditable and blocked when approval requirements are not met
platformChecklistItems.description: Successful and denied vendor use should emit reviewable records showing whether policy conditions were met.

seed.notes: Vendor use and denial conditions are recorded as audit events for later review.

evidence.title: Vendor access audit event inventory

evidence.title: Vendor use and denial event emission
evidence.description: convex/agentChatActions.ts records outbound vendor access used and denied events during chat generation workflows.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations remain responsible for their own vendor due diligence, contract review, and deciding whether the platform vendor set satisfies their subprocessor and business-associate requirements.

hipaa.title: Business associate contracts and other arrangements

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: A covered entity may permit a business associate to create, receive, maintain, or transmit electronic protected health information on the covered entity’s behalf only if the covered entity obtains satisfactory assurances, in accordance with § 164.314(a), that the business associate will appropriately safeguard the information. A covered entity is not required to obtain such satisfactory assurances from a business associate that is a subcontractor.
Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI held by the covered entity or business associate.

csf20.label: GV / GV.SC: Requirements to address cybersecurity risks in supply chains are established, prioritized, and integrated into contracts and other types of agreements with suppliers and other relevant third parties
GV / GV.SC: Planning and due diligence are performed to reduce risks before entering into formal supplier or other third-party relationships
ID / ID.AM: Inventories of software, services, and systems managed by the organization are maintained

soc2.label: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners.

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures information transmitted by the service is protected against unauthorized disclosure or modification in transit. The platform addresses that objective through HTTPS-oriented auth configuration, trusted-origin checks, and secure session transport settings, while certificate lifecycle and edge enforcement evidence remain outside this repo-backed workspace.
controls.controlStatement: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity is tracked as an active control in the platform register.

platformChecklistItems.label: HTTPS-oriented endpoint configuration is enforced
platformChecklistItems.description: Production auth configuration and trusted origins must require secure transport for hosted endpoints.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Auth URL and origin configuration review

seed.notes: Better Auth configuration fails closed unless production origins use HTTPS and trusted origins are explicitly allowed.

evidence.title: HTTPS Better Auth configuration
evidence.description: src/lib/server/env.server.ts requires BETTER_AUTH_URL and trusted origins to use HTTPS unless they point to loopback development.

platformChecklistItems.label: Secure session transport settings are enforced
platformChecklistItems.description: Session and temporary-link transport settings must align with secure transport expectations.

seed.notes: Session cookies are configured for secure transport on HTTPS origins and short-lived auth settings support controlled session handling.

evidence.title: Session transport configuration
evidence.description: convex/betterAuth/sharedOptions.ts enables secure cookies for HTTPS origins and convex/security.ts reports sessionExpiryHours, freshWindowMinutes, and temporaryLinkTtlMinutes.

platformChecklistItems.label: Certificate and edge operations are documented
platformChecklistItems.description: The hosted platform must retain evidence for certificate lifecycle management and edge enforcement.

seed.notes: Certificate lifecycle management and edge enforcement artifacts are not stored in the repo-backed control workspace yet.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for requiring secure access to the service within their own networks, browsers, devices, and downstream integrations.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to electronic protected health information that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.
Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted ePHI is not improperly modified without detection until disposed of.

csf20.label: PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected

soc2.label: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal to meet the entity’s objectives.

controls.title: Incident Handling
controls.implementationSummary: This control addresses incident handling expectations. In this workspace the platform only evidences investigation-supporting audit trails, exportable evidence, and retained review artifacts; substantive incident response procedures remain outside the hosted product evidence and are customer or provider program responsibilities.
controls.controlStatement: Implement an incident handling capability for incidents that is consistent with the incident response plan and includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery; Coordinate incident handling activities with contingency planning activities; Incorporate lessons learned from ongoing incident handling activities into incident response procedures, training, and testing, and implement the resulting changes accordingly; and Ensure the rigor, intensity, scope, and results of incident handling activities are comparable and predictable across the organization.

platformChecklistItems.label: Incident-supporting evidence can be exported
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should expose audit trails and evidence exports that support investigations.

seed.notes: Audit and evidence export flows can support incident investigations and post-incident analysis.

evidence.title: Evidence export support
evidence.description: src/routes/app/admin/security.tsx and convex/security.ts support evidence report generation and export.

platformChecklistItems.label: Investigation-supporting artifacts can be retained
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should retain hashes, review state, and exported evidence metadata that support post-incident analysis.

seed.notes: Evidence reports persist review metadata and integrity-linked export details that can be used during investigations.

evidence.title: Integrity-linked evidence reports
evidence.description: convex/security.ts stores contentHash, exportHash, exportIntegritySummary, and review metadata for generated evidence reports.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for incident detection, triage, escalation, containment, notification, and post-incident handling; the platform evidence here is only investigation-supporting material.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents.

csf20.label: RS / RS.AN: Analysis is performed to establish what has taken place during an incident and the root cause of the incident
RS / RS.CO: Internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents

controls.title: Continuous Monitoring
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures security-relevant posture signals are collected, summarized, and made available for recurring internal review. The platform supports that objective through a posture summary query, retained scan and retention records, audit-integrity telemetry, and evidence report generation from current monitoring state.
controls.controlStatement: Establishing the following system-level metrics to be monitored: {{ insert: param, ca-07_odp.01 }}; Establishing {{ insert: param, ca-07_odp.02 }} for monitoring and {{ insert: param, ca-07_odp.03 }} for assessment of control effectiveness; Ongoing control assessments in accordance with the continuous monitoring strategy; Ongoing monitoring of system and organization-defined metrics in accordance with the continuous monitoring strategy; Correlation and analysis of information generated by control assessments and monitoring; Response actions to address results of the analysis of control assessment and monitoring information; and Reporting the security and privacy status of the system to {{ insert: param, ca-7_prm_4 }} {{ insert: param, ca-7_prm_5 }}.

platformChecklistItems.label: Security posture signals are collected and summarized for ongoing review
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should collect and summarize current security posture signals that operators can review repeatedly.

seed.notes: The security posture summary aggregates authentication, scan, retention, backup, audit, telemetry, and vendor posture for review.

evidence.title: Admin summary dashboard
evidence.description: src/routes/app/admin/security.tsx renders posture summary cards for MFA, file inspection, audit integrity, retention jobs, and telemetry.

platformChecklistItems.label: Monitoring outputs include audit integrity, file scanning, retention, and backup signals
platformChecklistItems.description: Monitoring state should include the main classes of signals needed for security posture review and follow-up.

seed.notes: Monitoring outputs include audit integrity failures, document scan records, retention jobs, and backup verification records.

evidence.title: Monitoring evidence tables
evidence.description: convex/schema.ts defines documentScanEvents, retentionJobs, and backupVerificationReports used by posture and review workflows.

evidence.title: Audit integrity monitoring signal

platformChecklistItems.label: Reviewers can generate evidence reports from current monitoring state
platformChecklistItems.description: Current monitoring posture should be exportable into an evidence artifact suitable for recurring security review.

seed.notes: Reviewers can generate evidence reports from the current posture summary and supporting recent signals.

evidence.title: Evidence report generation action

evidence.title: Evidence report UI workflow
evidence.description: src/routes/app/admin/security.tsx exposes Generate evidence report and review/export actions for current monitoring state.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for establishing review cadence, assigning reviewers, and deciding how platform monitoring outputs are incorporated into broader organizational monitoring processes.

nist80066.label: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.

csf20.label: DE / DE.CM: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
DE / DE.AE: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities
DE / DE.AE: Information is correlated from multiple sources

soc2.label: COSO Principle 16: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and/or separate evaluations to ascertain whether the components of internal control are present and functioning.

controls.title: Authenticator Management
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures authenticators are managed, protected, and recoverable in a controlled manner. The platform supports that objective through stronger authenticators, recovery-related auditing, and guarded reset or account-recovery flows.
controls.controlStatement: Verifying, as part of the initial authenticator distribution, the identity of the individual, group, role, service, or device receiving the authenticator; Establishing initial authenticator content for any authenticators issued by the organization; Ensuring that authenticators have sufficient strength of mechanism for their intended use; Establishing and implementing administrative procedures for initial authenticator distribution, for lost or compromised or damaged authenticators, and for revoking authenticators; Changing default authenticators prior to first use; Changing or refreshing authenticators {{ insert: param, ia-05_odp.01 }} or when {{ insert: param, ia-05_odp.02 }} occur; Protecting authenticator content from unauthorized disclosure and modification; Requiring individuals to take, and having devices implement, specific controls to protect authenticators; and Changing authenticators for group or role accounts when membership to those accounts changes.

platformChecklistItems.label: Strong authenticators are available
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform must support strong authenticators such as passkeys or MFA factors.

seed.notes: The platform supports passkeys and built-in two-factor authentication for stronger authenticator management.

evidence.description: convex/betterAuth/sharedOptions.ts enables passkey and two-factor plugins, and profile UI exposes authenticator management flows.

platformChecklistItems.label: Recovery events are audited
platformChecklistItems.description: Password reset and recovery events must be logged for review.

seed.notes: Password reset request and completion events are defined for later review.

evidence.description: src/lib/shared/auth-audit.ts includes password_reset_requested and password_reset_completed event types.

platformChecklistItems.label: Recovery and authenticator changes are guarded
platformChecklistItems.description: Authenticator recovery and sign-in credential changes must be constrained by rate limits or fresh-session checks.

seed.notes: Sensitive auth endpoints are rate-limited and sign-in email changes require a fresh session.

evidence.title: Fresh-session change-email protection
evidence.description: convex/betterAuth/sharedOptions.ts blocks change-email unless the session satisfies the recent step-up freshness window.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for defining allowed credential types, approving recovery workflows, and governing exceptions to standard authentication policy.

nist80066.label: As reasonable and appropriate, train workforce members regarding procedures for:
HIPAA Standard: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed.

csf20.label: PR / PR.AA: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization
PR / PR.AA: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity. For those users whose access is administered by the entity, user system credentials are removed when user access is no longer authorized.

controls.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures audit records and related security findings can be reviewed, analyzed, and followed up through defined workflows. The platform provides audit history views, review-state retention, and evidence-report workflows that support that process, while formal operator review cadence and escalation procedures remain deployment-owned.
controls.controlStatement: Review and analyze system audit records {{ insert: param, au-06_odp.01 }} for indications of {{ insert: param, au-06_odp.02 }} and the potential impact of the inappropriate or unusual activity; Report findings to {{ insert: param, au-06_odp.03 }} ; and Adjust the level of audit record review, analysis, and reporting within the system when there is a change in risk based on law enforcement information, intelligence information, or other credible sources of information.

seed.notes: Evidence reports persist review status, notes, reviewer identity, and integrity-linked export data for later inspection.
