platformChecklistItems.description: Internal review cadence and escalation expectations must be documented for platform operators.

seed.notes: No operator-owned documented review cadence or escalation procedure is attached in the repo-backed control workspace yet.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for establishing review cadence, escalation paths, and documented follow-up for the evidence surfaced by the platform.

nist80066.label: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.
HIPAA Standard: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

csf20.label: DE / DE.AE: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities
DE / DE.AE: Information is correlated from multiple sources

soc2.label: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.

The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures.

controls.title: Configuration Change Control
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures security-relevant changes are made through controlled, reviewable, and reproducible mechanisms. The platform supports that objective through automated guardrail checks on Convex functions and reproducible compliance generation workflows, while formal approval records and operator change-management procedures are not yet fully evidenced in the repo-backed register.
controls.controlStatement: Determine and document the types of changes to the system that are configuration-controlled; Review proposed configuration-controlled changes to the system and approve or disapprove such changes with explicit consideration for security and privacy impact analyses; Document configuration change decisions associated with the system; Implement approved configuration-controlled changes to the system; Retain records of configuration-controlled changes to the system for {{ insert: param, cm-03_odp.01 }}; Monitor and review activities associated with configuration-controlled changes to the system; and Coordinate and provide oversight for configuration change control activities through {{ insert: param, cm-03_odp.02 }} that convenes {{ insert: param, cm-03_odp.03 }}.

platformChecklistItems.label: Security-relevant code and configuration changes follow a controlled change path
platformChecklistItems.description: Security-relevant changes should follow a defined, reviewable path rather than ad hoc mutation.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Change workflow and repo artifact review

seed.notes: The repo shows reproducible generation and validation workflows, but formal approval or change-review records are not attached in the control workspace yet.

evidence.title: Reproducible compliance refresh workflow
evidence.description: package.json defines compliance:refresh to regenerate the compliance artifacts from source rather than hand-editing outputs.

platformChecklistItems.label: Automated checks validate auth guardrails, function safety, and typed contracts
platformChecklistItems.description: Automated checks should validate authorization guardrails and type-level expectations for protected backend functions.

seed.notes: Automated checks scan exported Convex functions for approved auth wrappers and required return validators.

evidence.title: Convex function code-health audit
evidence.description: scripts/code-health-audit.ts classifies exported Convex functions, flags unprotected public functions, and fails when return validators are missing.

evidence.title: Auth-protected Convex builders
evidence.description: convex/auth/authorized.ts provides builder-level wrappers for site-admin and organization-scoped protected functions.

platformChecklistItems.label: Generated compliance artifacts are reproducible from source and refresh workflows
platformChecklistItems.description: Generated compliance outputs should be reproducible from tracked sources and scripted refresh workflows.

seed.notes: The active control register and related compliance artifacts are generated from source through scripted refresh commands.

evidence.title: Compliance refresh command chain
evidence.description: package.json defines compliance:refresh as a scripted chain that regenerates framework extracts and the active control register from source inputs.

evidence.title: Generated control register source of truth
evidence.description: scripts/compliance/generate-active-control-register.ts builds the active control register seed from framework extracts and local blueprint definitions.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for their own approval, CAB, and deployment control processes for any customer-managed integrations or operational changes outside the hosted service boundary.

nist80066.label: Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level to comply with §164.306(a)

csf20.label: PR / PR.PS: Configuration management practices are established and applied

soc2.label: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.

controls.title: System Monitoring
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures the service is monitored for indicators of attack, misuse, or operationally significant security events. The platform emits monitoring-relevant signals such as scan events, audit-integrity checks, and security posture summaries, but operator alert response procedures are not yet fully evidenced in this register.
controls.controlStatement: Monitor the system to detect: Attacks and indicators of potential attacks in accordance with the following monitoring objectives: {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.01 }} ; and Unauthorized local, network, and remote connections; Identify unauthorized use of the system through the following techniques and methods: {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.02 }}; Invoke internal monitoring capabilities or deploy monitoring devices: Strategically within the system to collect organization-determined essential information; and At ad hoc locations within the system to track specific types of transactions of interest to the organization; Analyze detected events and anomalies; Adjust the level of system monitoring activity when there is a change in risk to organizational operations and assets, individuals, other organizations, or the Nation; Obtain legal opinion regarding system monitoring activities; and Provide {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.03 }} to {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.04 }} {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.05 }}.

platformChecklistItems.label: Monitoring signals are emitted
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform must emit monitoring-relevant security signals for operational review.

seed.notes: Monitoring-related platform signals are exposed through document scan events, audit integrity checks, and security posture summaries.

evidence.title: Security monitoring signals
evidence.description: convex/security.ts reports document scan events, audit integrity failures, and telemetry posture.

platformChecklistItems.label: Operator alert response procedure is documented
platformChecklistItems.description: Internal alert routing and response expectations must be documented for the hosted platform.

seed.notes: No operator-owned monitoring response procedure is attached in the control workspace yet.

platformChecklistItems.label: Monitoring records are retained
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should retain monitoring outputs that investigators or operators can review after signals are generated.

seed.notes: Monitoring-related records are retained through document scan events, audit-integrity failures, and evidence report metadata.

evidence.title: Document scan event records
evidence.description: convex/schema.ts defines documentScanEvents and convex/security.ts reports their latest status, rejection counts, and quarantine counts.

evidence.title: Audit integrity failure records
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts emits audit_integrity_check_failed events when the audit log hash chain does not verify.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for reviewing service notifications, acting on customer-visible alerts, and integrating platform outputs into their own operational response processes.

nist80066.label: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.

csf20.label: DE / DE.CM: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
DE / DE.CM: External service provider activities and services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
DE / DE.AE: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities
DE / DE.AE: Information is correlated from multiple sources

soc2.label: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.
